rules: # Architecture
This is a Clean Architecture project (Domain <- Application <- Infrastructure <- Api). Never violate layer dependencies.
Domain layer has ZERO NuGet packages. Entities are pure POCOs with no EF Core attributes.
Controllers are thin dispatchers: only _mediator.Send() — no business logic, no repository injection.
Handlers in Infrastructure implement IRequestHandler and return Result<T>. Never throw exceptions for business failures.
All interfaces defined in Application, implemented in Infrastructure (Dependency Inversion).
# CQRS + MediatR
Commands = state-changing. Queries = read-only. Both are records implementing IRequest<Result<T>>.
Command naming: {Feature}{Action}Command. Query naming: Get{Entity}{Criteria}Query. Handler: {Name}Handler.
DTO naming: {Action}{Entity}Request/Response or {Entity}Dto. One validator per request DTO.
# Multi-Tenancy
Always filter queries by StationId unless user is Owner viewing consolidated data.
Use EF Core global query filters for tenant isolation. Owner role bypasses station filters.
# Authentication
JWT stored in HTTP-only cookies only — never Authorization header. Cookie names: access_token, refresh_token.
Refresh tokens are DB-backed, SHA256 hashed, with rotation. Reuse of revoked token = 401.
ICurrentUserService extracts claims. Never read HttpContext directly in handlers.
# Database
Tables: snake_case. PKs: id (UUID). FKs: {entity}_id. Timestamps: created_at, updated_at (TIMESTAMPTZ).
Soft deletes via is_active BOOLEAN. Never hard delete operational data.
EF Config section order: Table & Key -> Non-FK Properties -> Relationships -> Indexes -> Ignore.
Keep FK property definition with its relationship block. Add relationship type + delete behavior comments.
# Validation
FluentValidation in Application/Validators/ for structure/format. Business validation in handlers.
Frontend: Zod schemas mirror backend FluentValidation rules. Auth forms validate on submit only.
Pakistani phone: +92XXXXXXXXXX. Password: min 6 chars + at least 1 digit.
# Frontend
TanStack Query = server state. Zustand = client-only state. TanStack Form = form state. Never cross boundaries.
shadcn/ui for all base components. Always use cn() for className merging. CVA for variants.
routeTree.gen.ts is auto-generated — never edit it. Protected routes use beforeLoad() with redirect().
API client uses withCredentials: true. 401 interceptor handles refresh queue automatically.
# Cross-Cutting Rules
Always validate input at both frontend (Zod) AND backend (FluentValidation).
Always use DTOs for API communication — never expose domain entities.
Always check subscription plan limits before creating stations, users, or gated modules.
Always log sensitive actions: price changes, user creation, stock adjustments.
Never expose internal errors, stack traces, or implementation details to clients.
Response format: { success: true, data: {...} } on success. RFC 7807 Problem Details on error.
# SOLID in Practice
SRP: One controller per resource. One handler per command/query. One validator per DTO.
OCP: New features = new handlers, no changes to existing code. Feature flags via JSONB.
LSP: All entities are substitutable POCOs inheriting BaseEntity.
ISP: Focused repository interfaces — only methods the entity needs.
DIP: Application defines interfaces, Infrastructure implements them. Controllers depend on IMediator only.
# Business Rules
One open shift per station at a time. Opening meter >= last closing reading.
One active price per fuel type per station. Price changes require double-confirmation.
Stock = Opening Dip + Deliveries - Sales. Variance alerts on threshold breach.
Credit sales blocked at/above credit limit. Feature gating enforced at API level, not just UI.
